checks.name: Privileged Users adminCount1
checks.description: Misconfiguration: user accounts with adminCount=1 (protected object flag); review for inappropriate persistence of admin protection.
checks.remediation: Remove inappropriate adminSDHolder protection; relocate accounts to correct OUs with least-privilege delegation; investigate unexpected adminCount=1 on non-privileged users.

checks.name: Privileged Groups adminCount1
checks.description: Misconfiguration: security groups with adminCount=1; review membership and whether protection is expected.
checks.remediation: Validate each group is an intentional protected group; remove nested or unintended memberships; replace ad-hoc protected groups with delegated OU structure where possible.

checks.name: Privileged Computers adminCount1
checks.description: Misconfiguration: computer accounts with adminCount=1; validate each is an intentional protected object.
checks.remediation: Confirm Tier0/Tier1 systems only; remove adminCount=1 from non-DC servers that should not be adminSDHolder-protected; align with tiering model.

checks.name: Users with SIDHistory
checks.description: Exposure: user accounts with sIDHistory populated (migration or attack persistence); validate legitimacy.
checks.remediation: Remove stale sIDHistory values after migration cutover; investigate unexpected values; restrict who can write sIDHistory (e.g. domain admin only).

checks.name: Computers with RBCD
checks.description: Misconfiguration: resource-based constrained delegation (RBCD) on computers; review msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity.
checks.remediation: Clear msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity where not required; scope principals to least privilege; monitor changes to RBCD attributes.

checks.name: Users with RBCD
checks.description: Misconfiguration: RBCD on user accounts; review delegation to constrained targets.
checks.remediation: Remove RBCD from user objects unless required; prefer computer-based delegation patterns; audit who can modify these attributes.

checks.name: Kerberoastable Accounts
checks.description: Misconfiguration: user accounts with SPNs (Kerberoastable); review SPNs and use group managed service accounts where possible.
checks.remediation: Remove unnecessary SPNs from user accounts; prefer gMSA for services; enforce strong password policy for remaining SPN owners.

checks.name: Kerberoastable User Accounts
checks.description: Misconfiguration: Kerberoastable user accounts (SPN on user); overlaps conceptually with ACC-034 — use for Kerberos category reporting.
checks.remediation: Remove unnecessary SPNs from user accounts; prefer gMSA for services; enforce strong password policy for remaining SPN owners (same as ACC-034).

checks.name: AS-REP Roastable Accounts
checks.description: Misconfiguration: accounts with Kerberos pre-authentication disabled (AS-REP roastable).
checks.remediation: Enable Kerberos pre-authentication for affected accounts (or disable accounts that do not require interactive logon). Prefer group-based policies over per-account exceptions.

checks.name: Unconstrained Delegation Computers
checks.description: Misconfiguration: non-DC computers with unconstrained delegation (TGT exposure risk).
checks.remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation or constrain to required services; treat as Tier0 adjacent.

checks.name: Unconstrained Delegation Users
checks.description: Misconfiguration: user accounts with unconstrained delegation enabled.
checks.remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation from user accounts unless business-critical; use constrained or resource-based delegation; treat as credential theft risk.

checks.name: Constrained Delegation Computers
checks.description: Exposure: computers with constrained delegation (msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo); validate SPN targets.
checks.remediation: Restrict msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo to required SPNs; remove stale delegation; prefer resource-based delegation where possible.

checks.name: Constrained Delegation Users
checks.description: Exposure: users with constrained delegation; review allowed SPNs.
checks.remediation: Tighten allowed SPNs; remove user-based constrained delegation if not required; monitor changes to msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo.

checks.name: DES-Only Kerberos Users
checks.description: Weak crypto: accounts allowed DES-only Kerberos (UAC bit); disable DES for the domain and clear per-account use of DES-only.
checks.remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos at domain level; clear the Use Kerberos DES encryption types only flag on accounts; migrate legacy apps off DES.

checks.name: SYSVOL Policies insecure ACLs
checks.description: Misconfiguration: GPO folders under SYSVOL\\Policies where Everyone or Authenticated Users have Modify/Write/FullControl (review and tighten).
checks.remediation: Remove excessive Modify/Write/FullControl from SYSVOL\\Policies subfolders; grant Domain Admins/Creator Owner and required DC groups only; validate GPO delegation.

checks.name: ESC1 Enrollee-supplied SAN (certificate template)
checks.description: Certificate templates with CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT, authentication-capable EKU, no manager approval, and non-privileged enrollment: arbitrary SAN / impersonation risk.
checks.remediation: Remove EnrolleeSuppliesSubject where not required; enable manager approval; restrict enrollment ACL to least privilege.

checks.name: ESC2 Any Purpose or missing EKU (certificate template)
checks.description: Templates with Any Purpose EKU or no EKU, no manager approval, enrollable by non-privileged principals — master-key / CRA abuse risk.
checks.remediation: Restrict pKIExtendedKeyUsage; remove 2.5.29.37.0; enable manager approval.

checks.name: ESC3 Certificate Request Agent chain (templates)
checks.description: CRA template enrollable by non-privileged users and/or templates accepting CRA-signed requests — on-behalf enrollment / impersonation chain.
checks.remediation: Remove CRA EKU where not required; review msPKI-RA-Application-Policies; enable manager approval.

checks.name: ESC4 Weak template object ACLs
checks.description: Non-privileged principals with write/control on enabled certificate templates — can alter template flags (e.g. ESC1 conditions).
checks.remediation: Remove GenericWrite/WriteDacl/WriteOwner from non-admin principals on template objects.

checks.name: ESC5 Weak ACLs on critical PKI containers
checks.description: Non-privileged write access to NTAuth, Enrollment Services, AIA, CDP, OID, or PKI roots — trust chain and policy manipulation risk.
checks.remediation: Restrict write to Enterprise Admins / designated PKI admins; audit object access to CN=Public Key Services.

checks.name: ESC6 CA EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 (policy module)
checks.description: Enterprise CA policy may allow arbitrary SAN in requests (certutil EditFlags). Requires RPC access to CA or running on CA-adjacent host.
checks.remediation: Disable EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2; apply KB5014754; validate StrongCertificateBindingEnforcement.

checks.name: ESC7 Weak ACLs on Enterprise CA object
checks.description: Non-privileged GenericWrite/WriteDacl on pKIEnrollmentService — maps to CA management abuse (ManageCA / officer workflows).
checks.remediation: Tighten CA object ACL in AD and certsrv.msc security; align with tier model.

checks.name: ESC8 Web Enrollment over HTTP / relay surface
checks.description: HTTP Web Enrollment or NTLM-exposed certsrv increases relay risk; HTTPS-only still needs EPA verification.
checks.remediation: Require HTTPS; enable EPA on IIS certsrv; restrict NTLM; see KB5005413.

checks.name: Certificate templates (configuration partition listing)
checks.description: Full LDAP listing of certificate template objects. Use for inventory and to scope ADCS-ESC reviews. Each row is a PKI object, not proof of vulnerability by itself.
checks.remediation: Use results for inventory and change tracking. Prioritize templates with client-auth EKU and weak enrollment ACLs using ADCS-ESC1–ESC8.

checks.name: Templates Allowing Client Authentication
checks.description: Certificate templates that include client authentication EKU (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2). These are common targets for domain authentication abuse when combined with weak enrollment or subject/SAN settings.
checks.remediation: Restrict enrollment; require manager approval where appropriate; ensure subject/SAN flags and msPKI settings align with least privilege; cross-check ADCS-ESC1–ESC3.

checks.name: Templates Allowing Smart Card Logon
checks.description: Templates granting smart card logon EKU (1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.2). Mis-issued smart card logon certs can support credential abuse paths; review issuance and physical controls.
checks.remediation: Limit enrollment; validate key archival and issuance policies; ensure smart card issuance aligns with tier model.

checks.name: Templates Allowing PKINIT Client Auth
checks.description: Templates with PKINIT client authentication EKU (1.3.6.1.5.2.3.4). Certificates can be used in Kerberos PKINIT flows; combined with weak enrollment they raise domain authentication risk.
checks.remediation: Tighten enrollment ACLs; review PKINIT usage; validate KDC and domain controller certificate binding settings.

checks.name: Templates Allowing Code Signing
checks.description: Templates allowing code signing EKU (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.3). Abuse can support malware signing or policy bypass if enrollment is too broad.
checks.remediation: Restrict code signing template enrollment to designated groups; audit issued certificates.

checks.name: Templates Without Manager Approval
checks.description: Templates without manager approval (CT_FLAG_PEND_ALL_REQUESTS not set in msPKI-Enrollment-Flag). Speeds issuance and enlarges the window for mis-issued certificates when other controls fail.
checks.remediation: Enable manager approval for sensitive templates; combine with restricted enrollment and ADCS-ESC reviews.

checks.name: Templates Without Authorized Signatures Required
checks.description: Templates with zero or missing required RA signatures (msPKI-RA-Signature). Weakens issuance workflow and can ease abuse when enrollment is misconfigured.
checks.remediation: Require RA signatures where policy demands; align with PKI officer workflows.

checks.name: Enterprise Certificate Authorities
checks.description: Enterprise CA enrollment service objects (pKIEnrollmentService) in AD. Lists published CAs and template bindings; use for mapping attack surface alongside ADCS-ESC6–ESC8.
checks.remediation: Validate CA host hardening, Web Enrollment, and RPC exposure; review certificateTemplates published per CA.

checks.name: NTAuth Certificate Store
checks.description: NTAuthCertificates store in AD lists CAs trusted for domain authentication. Unexpected or stale entries can affect trust decisions.
checks.remediation: Keep NTAuth aligned with intentional enterprise roots; remove retired CAs after migration.

checks.name: Trusted Root CAs in AD
checks.description: Certification authority objects under the enterprise PKI tree. Broad listing helps identify roots and intermediates published to the forest.
checks.remediation: Audit CA certificates for lifecycle and trust alignment; remove obsolete publishing.

checks.name: Templates with No Expiration Validation
checks.description: Templates missing pKIExpirationPeriod (or equivalent validity constraints visible in LDAP). May indicate legacy or inconsistent template definitions worth validating in CA policy.
checks.remediation: Define and enforce validity periods per template; validate on issuing CA.

checks.name: Templates Allowing Key Archival
checks.description: Templates with key archival enrollment flag set. Increases sensitivity: archived keys require strict access controls on KRA and CA database.
checks.remediation: Verify KRA configuration, ACLs, and auditing; limit templates with archival to required scenarios.

checks.name: Templates with Exportable Private Key
checks.description: Templates allowing exportable private keys (msPKI-Private-Key-Flag). Eases credential theft if certificates are issued to interactive users or broadly enrolled.
checks.remediation: Disable exportable keys where not required; prefer hardware protection for high-value use cases.

checks.name: Templates for Machine Certificates
checks.description: Templates with machine/enrollment name flags indicating machine certificate scenarios. Review for unintended domain computer authentication surface.
checks.remediation: Restrict enrollment to intended device OUs and groups; validate autoenrollment GPO scope.

checks.name: Templates Allowing Renewal with Different Key
checks.description: Templates allowing renewal with a new key pair. Can complicate revocation and lifecycle tracking if combined with weak identity binding.
checks.remediation: Ensure renewal policies and revocation workflows cover key-change renewals.

checks.name: Accounts with Certificate Published
checks.description: User accounts with userCertificate populated. Published certs may support mapping or smart card scenarios; unexpected entries warrant review for stale or over-published credentials.
checks.remediation: Validate intentional publication; remove obsolete certificates from the directory.

checks.name: Computers with Certificate Published
checks.description: Computer accounts with userCertificate populated. May reflect domain controller or service certs published to AD; unexpected hosts warrant validation.
checks.remediation: Confirm published certs match intended roles; rotate and clean up stale entries.

checks.name: CRL Distribution Points
checks.description: CRL distribution point objects in the configuration partition. Use to map revocation publication points and HTTP/S surface.
checks.remediation: Ensure CDP URLs are reachable and HTTPS where required; monitor expiration and replication.

checks.name: [DACL review] Certificate templates (nTSecurityDescriptor for enrollment ACL analysis)
checks.description: Lists templates with security descriptor loaded. LDAP cannot resolve 'Everyone' enrollment in one filter—manually review DACL for Authenticated Users / Domain Users enrollment. Prefer ADCS-ESC4 for write-equivalent abuse on templates.
checks.remediation: Audit enrollment ACEs on each template; remove broad enrollment where inappropriate; correlate with ADCS-ESC4.

checks.name: Offline Root CA References
checks.description: CA objects referencing offline roots or cross-boundary trust material. Use for PKI topology documentation and continuity planning.
checks.remediation: Validate CRL/AIA publication for offline roots; document trust chains.

checks.name: AIA Authority Information Access Objects
checks.description: AIA container under Public Key Services. Authority Information Access publication affects chain building for clients.
checks.remediation: Ensure AIA URLs are correct, reachable, and aligned with issuance hierarchy.

checks.name: Templates with Subject Name from AD
checks.description: Templates with subject name from Active Directory (CT_FLAG_SUBJECT_ALT_REQUIRE_UPN or related name flags in msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag). Affects how subjects are bound to directory identities.
checks.remediation: Validate name flags against intended use; cross-check with ESC1-style SAN issues.

checks.name: [Listing] Template schema version fields (all templates)
checks.description: Lists all templates with schema/minor revision attributes. Useful for spotting legacy versions; not a vulnerability by itself.
checks.remediation: Deprecate legacy templates; migrate to supported schema versions with explicit EKU and ACLs.

checks.name: [Listing] Certificate templates (full forest set; manual ESC review)
checks.description: Broad listing only—same objects as a full template inventory. Use to drive manual review; does not imply vulnerable configuration without ADCS-ESC and ACL analysis.
checks.remediation: Cross-reference with ADCS-ESC1–ESC8 and per-template ACL review; retire unused templates.

checks.name: [Listing] Templates for enrollment rights review (full set)
checks.description: Lists every template with timestamps—LDAP does not enumerate enrollment ACEs in this filter. Use for workflow tracking; validate enrollment with ADCS-ESC4 and DACL review.
checks.remediation: Verify only intended groups may enroll; remove excessive ACEs.

checks.name: Client authentication templates (same LDAP scope as CERT-006)
checks.description: Intentionally the same filter as CERT-006 (client authentication EKU). Retained for checklist parity; findings overlap—do not double-count risk. Scoring suppressed.
checks.remediation: Use CERT-006 or ADCS-ESC checks for reporting; disable one ID in overrides if duplicates are undesirable.

checks.name: Certificate Authority Health
checks.description: Enterprise CA objects with timestamps for health-style review. LDAP alone does not prove outage; use with monitoring and certutil/CA logs.
checks.remediation: Monitor CA services, CRL validity, and disk space on CA hosts.

checks.name: CRL Distribution Point Availability
checks.description: CRL distribution points with change metadata. Supports tracking revocation publication changes.
checks.remediation: Validate CRL publication schedules and HTTP/S endpoints.

checks.name: OCSP Responder Status
checks.description: Enterprise CA objects relevant to OCSP deployment review. LDAP does not prove OCSP responder health—follow with endpoint tests.
checks.remediation: Deploy and monitor OCSP responders per Microsoft guidance; align AIA/OCSP URLs.

checks.name: [Listing] Templates for version drift review (full set)
checks.description: Lists all templates—LDAP does not detect 'mismatch' without comparing to published CA policy. Manual review only.
checks.remediation: Consolidate templates; disable superseded definitions.

checks.name: [Listing] Templates for autoenrollment policy alignment (full set)
checks.description: Broad template listing for GPO/autoenrollment correlation. LDAP alone does not read autoenrollment GPOs.
checks.remediation: Scope autoenrollment GPOs tightly; review templates granted autoenrollment.

checks.name: Templates with Certificate Request Agent EKU
checks.description: Templates publishing the CRA EKU (1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.1). Overlaps ADCS-ESC3—validate enrollment and manager approval.
checks.remediation: Remove CRA EKU where unnecessary; enforce manager approval and enrollment restrictions.

checks.name: Web Enrollment Security
checks.description: Enterprise CA objects tied to Web Enrollment attack surface. Correlates with ADCS-ESC8 for HTTP/NTLM exposure—this LDAP check does not replace network verification.
checks.remediation: Require HTTPS, harden IIS certsrv, restrict NTLM; see KB5005413.

checks.name: Templates with supersession reference (msPKI-Template-Supersede)
checks.description: Templates that reference another template in the supersession chain. Validate whether superseded templates are disabled where policy requires.
checks.remediation: Disable superseded templates after migration; enforce supersession links.

checks.name: CA Certificate Expiration
checks.description: Enterprise CAs for CA certificate lifetime review. Expired or soon-to-expire CA certs break chain validation.
checks.remediation: Plan CA cert renewal; monitor AIA/CRL publication during rollover.

checks.name: Templates with minimal key size at or below 1024 bits (LDAP)
checks.description: Templates advertising msPKI-Minimal-Key-Size ≤ 1024. Modern baselines typically require ≥2048; confirm algorithm context on issuing CA.
checks.remediation: Raise minimum key size on templates and CAs; reissue where non-compliant.

checks.name: [Listing] Template objects for validity period review (LDAP cannot compare periods)
checks.description: Broad listing: LDAP does not decode pKIExpirationPeriod for numeric comparison in this check. Manual or CA-policy review required for 'excessive' validity.
checks.remediation: Reduce validity periods to operational minimums; enforce renewal discipline.

checks.name: CA Audit Settings
checks.description: Enterprise CAs for audit configuration review. LDAP listing does not read local CA audit policy—use CA host baselines.
checks.remediation: Enable and forward CA audit logs; align with SIEM coverage.

checks.name: [DACL review] Templates with security descriptor (ACL triage)
checks.description: Loads nTSecurityDescriptor for manual or downstream ACL review. Prefer ADCS-ESC4 for automated weak-ACL detection.
checks.remediation: Remove write-equivalent rights for non-PKI admins; validate ADCS-ESC4 output.

checks.name: CA Database Backup Status
checks.description: Enterprise CAs for backup posture review. AD LDAP does not prove backup success—follow with backup reports.
checks.remediation: Back up CA database and keys per Microsoft DR guidance; test restore.

checks.name: Certificate Revocation Reasons
checks.description: Enterprise CAs for revocation workflow documentation. Pair with operational CRL/OCSP monitoring.
checks.remediation: Maintain revocation reason policies and responder availability.

checks.name: [Listing] Smart card logon template review (full set; see CERT-007 for EKU-specific)
checks.description: Legacy stub used a broad template list. For EKU-specific smart card templates use CERT-007. This row is listing-oriented; scoring suppressed.
checks.remediation: Validate smart card templates, KDC certs, and user mappings.

checks.name: Templates with Any Purpose or missing EKU (ESC2-style LDAP signal)
checks.description: Templates with Any Purpose EKU or no EKU—high-risk configuration class. Overlaps ADCS-ESC2; use ESC2 for full logic.
checks.remediation: Explicitly set required EKUs; remove Any Purpose where not justified.

checks.name: CA Role Separation
checks.description: Enterprise CAs for CA role separation review. Combined CA admin + backup operator on same accounts increases insider risk.
checks.remediation: Separate CA administration per Microsoft PKI best practices.

checks.name: [Listing] Subject name policy review (all templates)
checks.description: Broad listing for subject/SAN flag review. Prefer targeted checks (e.g. CERT-029, ADCS-ESC1) for specific misconfigurations.
checks.remediation: Tighten subject name flags; disable enrollee-supplied subject where not needed.

checks.name: CA Security Descriptor
checks.description: Enterprise CA objects for security descriptor review. Weak ACLs on pKIEnrollmentService relate to ADCS-ESC7—use LDAP plus ESC7 for full analysis.
checks.remediation: Restrict ManageCA and related rights to PKI admins; validate with ADCS-ESC7.

checks.name: Dangerous ACE: GenericAll (non-privileged principal)
checks.description: Objects where a non-baseline principal has Allow GenericAll (full control). Review delegation and group memberships.
checks.remediation: Remove unnecessary GenericAll; prefer more specific rights; validate nested groups and shadow admins.

checks.name: Dangerous ACE: GenericWrite (non-privileged principal)
checks.description: Objects where a non-baseline principal has Allow GenericWrite (broad write access). Often enables property takeover or lateral movement.
checks.remediation: Tighten ACLs; remove GenericWrite from low-trust principals; review OU and GPO delegation.

checks.name: Dangerous ACE: WriteDacl (non-privileged principal)
checks.description: Objects where a non-baseline principal can modify the DACL (grant self further rights).
checks.remediation: Restrict WriteDacl to Domain Admins / delegated PKI or GPO owners as appropriate; audit nested groups.

checks.name: Dangerous ACE: WriteOwner (non-privileged principal)
checks.description: Objects where a non-baseline principal can take ownership (often leading to full control).
checks.remediation: Remove WriteOwner from untrusted principals; validate sensitive OUs and GPOs.

checks.name: Broad ACE: WriteProperty (non-privileged principal)
checks.description: Objects with Allow WriteProperty for non-baseline principals (noisy; includes targeted property writes). Review ObjectType when present.
checks.remediation: Narrow rights to required properties; prefer validated admin paths.

checks.name: DCs with SMB Signing Disabled (SYSVOL GPO)

gptTmplRules.detail: GPO explicitly sets SMB server RequireSecuritySignature to disabled (0). Read from SYSVOL SecEdit GptTmpl.inf (domain user readable).

checks.description: Scans Group Policy SecEdit templates on SYSVOL for SMB server packet signing. Does not use remote registry; requires read access to \\domain\\SYSVOL like any domain-joined client.
checks.remediation: Set SMB server signing to required via GPO (or remove weak values); validate with security baseline.

checks.name: DCs LDAP Signing Not Required (SYSVOL GPO)

gptTmplRules.detail: GPO sets LDAPServerIntegrity to 0/1 (none or negotiate). Value 2 requires signing.

checks.description: Detects weak LDAP signing policy pushed via GPO registry values in GptTmpl.inf (not a live DC registry probe).
checks.remediation: Require LDAP signing on domain controllers; set LDAPServerIntegrity to required in GPO.

checks.name: DCs LDAP Channel Binding Disabled (SYSVOL GPO)

gptTmplRules.detail: GPO sets LDAP channel binding enforcement to disabled (0).

checks.description: Reads ldapEnforceChannelBinding from SYSVOL GptTmpl.inf when present. Matches either NTDS or LSA policy paths under [Registry Values].
checks.remediation: Enable LDAP channel binding (EPA) per Microsoft guidance for DCs and clients.

checks.name: LDAP Signing Requirements (SYSVOL GPO)

gptTmplRules.detail: GPO sets LDAPServerIntegrity to 0/1 (not require signing).

checks.description: Domain-wide LDAP signing posture via GPO SecEdit templates on SYSVOL.
checks.remediation: Require LDAP signing for domain controllers.

checks.name: LDAP Channel Binding (SYSVOL GPO)

gptTmplRules.detail: GPO disables LDAP channel binding enforcement.

checks.description: SYSVOL-based detection of ldapEnforceChannelBinding=0 in GptTmpl.inf.
checks.remediation: Enable channel binding enforcement for LDAP over TLS.

checks.name: SMB Signing Configuration (SYSVOL GPO)

gptTmplRules.detail: SMB server signing not required by GPO (0).
SMB client signing not required by GPO (0).

checks.description: Evaluates SMB signing-related registry values as deployed via GPO SecEdit (SYSVOL), not live registry on endpoints.
checks.remediation: Require SMB signing on clients and servers per baseline.

checks.name: NTLM Authentication Settings (SYSVOL GPO)

gptTmplRules.detail: LmCompatibilityLevel below 3 (send NTLMv2 responses only / refuse LM). Review domain baseline.

checks.description: Reads LmCompatibilityLevel from SYSVOL GptTmpl.inf [Registry Values] when administrators deploy it via security templates.
checks.remediation: Set LAN Manager authentication level to send NTLMv2 responses only (typically 3-5).

checks.name: SMBv1 Enabled (SYSVOL GPO)
checks.description: Detects SMB1 enabled via GPO-deployed registry values in SecEdit templates.
checks.remediation: Disable SMBv1 via GPO and Server Manager features.

checks.name: SMB Signing Not Required (SYSVOL GPO)

gptTmplRules.detail: Workstation GPO does not require SMB signing (0).

checks.description: Client-side SMB signing requirement as expressed in GptTmpl.inf. Server-side coverage is in DC-033 / DCONF-011.
checks.remediation: Require SMB signing for workstations and servers.

checks.name: Forest Functional Level
checks.description: Informational inventory: forest / functional-level related objects in the configuration partition. Not a pass/fail misconfiguration by itself; excluded from risk scoring.
checks.remediation: Use only for reporting and change tracking. Do not treat matches as automatic vulnerabilities. Exclude from executive risk rollups if your workflow scores only LDAP risk checks.

checks.name: Schema Master (dMD FSMO holder)
checks.description: Informational inventory: schema partition root (dMD) and Schema Master FSMO holder. Excluded from risk scoring.
checks.remediation: Use for documentation and DR planning (who holds Schema Master). Not a security finding by itself; remove from risk dashboards if only misconfiguration checks should appear.

checks.name: Example: all user objects (domain partition)
checks.description: Demo inventory: lists enabled user objects in the domain partition. For learning and query validation only; excluded from risk scoring.
checks.remediation: Do not use as a security control. Replace with targeted risk checks (e.g. stale accounts, weak UAC flags) for assessments; omit from risk summaries.

checks.name: Example: schema partition root search
checks.description: Demo inventory: one-level search at the schema naming context root. For documentation and tooling examples only; excluded from risk scoring.
checks.remediation: Use only to validate connectivity and schema NC visibility. Not a vulnerability; exclude from automated risk scoring pipelines.
